skill.name: medication-adherence-message-gen
skill.description: Generates personalized medication reminder messages using behavioral psychology principles including social norms, loss aversion, and implementation intentions. Supports English and Chinese output with customizable tone and format.
skill.summary: Generate psychology-backed medication reminder messages for improved patient adherence
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 78 patterns across 5 files (1189 lines including weak cryptographic algorithm references, system reconnaissance keywords, shell backtick execution, path traversal, and hardcoded URLs. AI evaluation confirmed all 78 findings are false positives: 'MD' matches are medication/documentation text not MD5 hashes, backticks are Markdown inline code formatting, '../' appears only in a security checklist warning against path traversal, and the single hardcoded URL is a legitimate source attribution link. The skill is a self-contained Python script that generates medication reminder messages with no network calls, no file writes, no dynamic code execution, and no data exfiltration. Semantic review found no prompt injection attempts, no credential harvesting, and no hidden malicious intent. A prior independent audit gave this skill a Production Ready grade of 85/100.

content.value_statement: Generates personalized medication reminder messages using eight behavioral psychology principles. Helps healthcare providers and researchers create effective, psychology-backed patient communication for improved medication adherence.
actual_capabilities: Generate personalized medication reminder messages in English and Chinese
Apply eight behavioral psychology principles to craft persuasive message content
Customize message tone across gentle, firm, encouraging, and urgent styles
Output messages in plain text or structured JSON format
Provide alternative message variations for A/B testing or patient preference
Include psychology insight explanations with each generated message
limitations: Does not send actual SMS or push notifications to patients
Message templates are pre-written and not dynamically generated by AI
Limited to English and Chinese language support
Requires Python 3.10 or later to execute the bundled script

use_cases.title: Clinical Research Messaging
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher generates standardized medication reminder messages for an adherence study, applying consistent psychological principles across multiple patient cohorts.

use_cases.title: Digital Health App Integration
use_cases.description: A health app developer uses the message templates to add psychology-backed personalized reminders to a patient-facing mobile application.

use_cases.title: Academic Writing on Patient Communication
use_cases.description: A medical academic uses the skill to explore how behavioral psychology principles translate into practical patient communication strategies for research papers.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Reminder Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a medication reminder for a patient taking Metformin after breakfast. Use a gentle tone and English language.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple reminder message

prompt_templates.title: Psychology Principle Application
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a medication adherence message for Mr. Zhang who takes Atorvastatin 20mg after dinner. Use the social norms principle with an encouraging tone in Chinese.
prompt_templates.scenario: Applying a specific behavioral psychology principle

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Variant Message Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate three alternative reminder messages for a patient on antihypertensive medication. Use the loss aversion principle, firm tone, English language, and output as JSON.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating multiple message variants for testing

prompt_templates.title: Principle Effectiveness Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze which behavioral psychology principle would be most effective for a patient with historically low medication adherence. Generate a complete message set with all four tones and explain the psychological reasoning behind each variation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced analysis combining psychology and message generation

output_examples.input: Generate a reminder for Metformin, patient John, encouraging tone, English
output_examples.output: A personalized medication reminder message using encouraging tone with a psychology insight explaining which principle was applied and why.

output_examples.input: Create a reminder with social norms principle, Chinese, gentle tone
output_examples.output: A Chinese-language reminder emphasizing that most patients adhere to their medication schedule, with alternative phrasings in different tones.

output_examples.input: Generate JSON output with multiple principle variations for Atorvastatin
output_examples.output: Structured JSON containing the main reminder message, psychology insight description, and up to three alternative message variations.

best_practices: Always specify the medication name and preferred language for accurate and culturally appropriate message generation
Test multiple psychology principles to find the most effective messaging approach for your specific patient population
Review all generated messages for medical accuracy and cultural appropriateness before sending to patients
anti_patterns: Do not use generated messages in clinical settings without review by qualified healthcare professionals
Avoid using urgent tones for routine medication reminders as this may cause unnecessary patient anxiety
Do not assume one psychology principle works universally across all patient demographics without testing

faq.question: What psychology principles does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill supports eight behavioral psychology principles: social norms, loss aversion, implementation intentions, immediate rewards, commitment consistency, self-efficacy, anchoring effect, and scarcity.

faq.question: Can this skill send SMS messages directly to patients?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates message copy. You need a separate SMS gateway or messaging service to deliver messages to patients.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports English and Chinese (Simplified). Templates are available in both languages with culturally appropriate phrasing for each.

faq.question: Can I customize the message templates?
faq.answer: Yes, the message templates in scripts/main.py can be edited to add new phrasing or adapt messages to specific cultural and clinical contexts.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for clinical use?
faq.answer: The skill generates message copy informed by behavioral psychology research. All messages should be reviewed by qualified healthcare professionals before any clinical use.

faq.question: How do I run the medication reminder generator?
faq.answer: Run python scripts/main.py -m 'MedicationName' with optional parameters for patient name, dosage, time, psychology principle, tone, and language.
